devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । एवमेतत्पुरा पृष्टो मैत्रेयो भगवान् किल । क्षत्त्रा वनं प्रविष्टेन त्यक्त्वा स्वगृह ऋद्धिमत् ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca evametatpurā pṛṣṭo maitreyo bhagavān kila kṣattrā vanaṃ praviṣṭena tyaktvā svagṛha ṛddhimat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
[1] It is reported that formerly, venerable Maitreya[2] was asked this very question, in the same manner by Vidura who took to forest, after leaving his affluent house—the house, wherein Lord Kṛṣṇa, the ruler of all, (but as) the ambassador (of Pāṇḍavas) entered (and stayed) as if it were bis own, after leaving the palace of the Paurava King (Duryodhana).
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā interprets: Formerly the venerable Vidura who was the advisor of Pāṇḍavas and was an aṃśa of Yamadharma, the controller of all beings, left the house of Dhṛtarāṣṭra which he considered as his own and took to forest. It is reported that when Vidura left his own magnificent house and went to the jungle, he asked the venerable sage Maitreya the same question, in the same way.
Bhāgavata Candrikā adds that leaving his own house as well as that of the Paurava King shows the physical and mental renunciation of Vidura.

Footnote [2]:
Maitreya or Kauśārava A son of Kuśārava and Mitrā and disciple of Parāśara; met Kṛṣṇa on the eve of his departure to Heaven and was ordered to be the preceptor of Vidura.—DHM. 195, Purāṇa Index. 2.739-40.

devanagari: यद्वा अयं मंत्रकृद्वो भगवान् अखिलेश्वरः । पौरवेन्द्रगृहं हित्वा प्रविवेशात्मसात्कृतम् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: yadvā ayaṃ maṃtrakṛdvo bhagavān akhileśvaraḥ pauravendragṛhaṃ hitvā praviveśātmasātkṛtam

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
[1] It is reported that formerly, venerable Maitreya[2] was asked this very question, in the same manner by Vidura who took to forest, after leaving his affluent house—the house, wherein Lord Kṛṣṇa, the ruler of all, (but as) the ambassador (of Pāṇḍavas) entered (and stayed) as if it were bis own, after leaving the palace of the Paurava King (Duryodhana).
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā interprets: Formerly the venerable Vidura who was the advisor of Pāṇḍavas and was an aṃśa of Yamadharma, the controller of all beings, left the house of Dhṛtarāṣṭra which he considered as his own and took to forest. It is reported that when Vidura left his own magnificent house and went to the jungle, he asked the venerable sage Maitreya the same question, in the same way.
Bhāgavata Candrikā adds that leaving his own house as well as that of the Paurava King shows the physical and mental renunciation of Vidura.

Footnote [2]:
Maitreya or Kauśārava A son of Kuśārava and Mitrā and disciple of Parāśara; met Kṛṣṇa on the eve of his departure to Heaven and was ordered to be the preceptor of Vidura.—DHM. 195, Purāṇa Index. 2.739-40.

devanagari: राजोवाच । कुत्र क्षत्तुर्भगवता मैत्रेयेणास सङ्गमः । कदा वा सहसंवाद एतद् वर्णय नः प्रभो ॥ ३ ॥

translation:
The King said:
Oh Lord! Tell us in details, at what place did the meeting of Vidura with venerable Maitreya take place? and when (or what topic) did they discuss?

devanagari: न ह्यल्पार्थोदयस्तस्य विदुरस्य अमलात्मनः । तस्मिन् वरीयसि प्रश्नः साधुवादोपबृंहितः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: na hyalpārthodayastasya vidurasya amalātmanaḥ tasmin varīyasi praśnaḥ sādhuvādopabṛṃhitaḥ
translation: For, the question posed by Vidura, of pure Soul, to the great (sage) cannot be of less importance—especially so when it was praised as a good question by Maitreya (himself).

devanagari: सूत उवाच । स एवं ऋषिवर्योऽयं पृष्टो राज्ञा परीक्षिता । प्रत्याह तं सुबहुवित् प्रीतात्मा श्रूयतामिति ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: sūta uvāca sa evaṃ ṛṣivaryo pratyāha taṃ subahuvit prītātmā śrūyatāmiti

translation:
Sūta said:
When the great sage, master (lit. knower) of different branches of learning, was thus asked by king Parīkṣit, he, being very pleased, replied, “Listen (I shall narrate to you).

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । यदा तु राजा स्वसुतानसाधून् । पुष्णन् न धर्मेण विनष्टदृष्टिः । भ्रातुर्यविष्ठस्य सुतान् विबन्धून् । प्रवेश्य लाक्षाभवने ददा...

translation: When the blind king Dhṛtarāṣṭra supported in an unrighteous manner, his wicked sons, and deceitfully made the relative-less (fatherless) children of his younger brother, to live (lit. enter) the house of lac and set it on fire.

devanagari: यदा सभायां कुरुदेवदेव्याः । केशाभिमर्शं सुतकर्म गर्ह्यम् । न वारयामास नृपः स्नुषायाः । स्वास्रैर्हरन्त्याः कुचकुङ्कुमानि ॥ ७ ॥

translation: When king Dhṛtarāṣṭra did not prevent his son from censurable act of dragging (lit. touching) the hair of the queen of Kuru king (Yuḍhiṣṭhira), his daughter-in-law, whose tears washed away the saffron on her breasts [Or, which was the cause of subsequent washing away of the saffron on breasts of the wives of the enemies. ]

devanagari: द्यूते त्वधर्मेण जितस्य साधोः । सत्यावलंबस्य वनं गतस्य । न याचतोऽदात् समयेन दायं । तमोजुषाणो यदजातशत्रोः ॥ ८ ॥

translation: As he succumbed to infatuation (or thoughtlessness, or serving his son), he did not return, as per terms of agreement, his due patrimony to the pious Yudhiṣṭhira who always followed the path of truth, and was fraudulently defeated in gambling, though he (Yuḍhiṣṭhira) respectfully requested for it (his share), after his return from the forest.

devanagari: यदा च पार्थप्रहितः सभायां । जगद्‍गुरुर्यानि जगाद कृष्णः । न तानि पुंसां अमृतायनानि । राजोरु मेने क्षतपुण्यलेशः ॥ ९ ॥

translation: When the king whose (remaining small) portion of merit was spent up, did not regard as important the speeches of Kṛṣṇa, the Master of the world, (when) deputed by Pāṇḍavas,—speeches delivered in the (royal) assembly, and which were as if nectar-oozing to men (like Bhīṣma).

devanagari: यदोपहूतो भवनं प्रविष्टो । मंत्राय पृष्टः किल पूर्वजेन । अथाह तन्मंत्रदृशां वरीयान् । यन्मंत्रिणो वैदुरिकं वदन्ति ॥ १० ॥

translation: When Viḍura was invited by his elder brother for consultation, he went to the palace. What he (Vidura), prominent amongst counsellors, proffered as an advice came later to be called ‘Vidura’s Advice’ (Vidura-Nīti) by (political) advisors.

devanagari: अजातशत्रोः प्रतियच्छ दायं । तितिक्षतो दुर्विषहं तवागः । सहानुजो यत्र वृकोदराहिः । श्वसन् रुषा यत्त्वमलं बिभेषि ॥ ११ ॥

translation:
(Viḍura advised)
“Return the hereditary share (of kingdom) to Yudhiṣṭhira who has suffered unbearable wrongs from you. It is against these that the serpent in the form of Bhīma of whom you are so much afraid, is hissing in rage along with his brothers.

devanagari: पार्थांस्तु देवो भगवान् मुकुन्दो । गृहीतवान् स क्षितिदेवदेवः । आस्ते स्वपुर्यां यदुदेवदेवो । विनिर्जिताशेष नृदेवदेवः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: pārthāṃstu devo bhagavān mukundo gṛhītavān sa kṣitidevadevaḥ āste svapuryāṃ yadudevadevo vinirjitāśeṣa nṛdevadevaḥ
translation: The God, Lord Kṛṣṇa, along with Brāhmaṇas and gods, has taken (the side of) Pāṇḍavas. The Lord of the Yadus who has subdued all kings of kings, stays in his own city.

devanagari: स एष दोषः पुरुषद्विडास्ते । गृहान् प्रविष्टो यमपत्यमत्या । पुष्णासि कृष्णाद्विमुखो गतश्रीः । त्यजाश्वशैवं कुलकौशलाय ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: sa eṣa doṣaḥ puruṣadviḍāste gṛhān praviṣṭo yamapatyamatyā puṣṇāsi kṛṣṇādvimukho gataśrīḥ tyajāśvaśaivaṃ kulakauśalāya

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active second single], [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: You, who have lost prosperity due to your aversion to Kṛṣṇa, are nourishing Duryodhana with the idea that he is your child. But he, the hater of the Supreme Man, is an evil incarnate which has entered your house and is staying. For the good of the family (at least) remove that inauspicious person.”

devanagari: इति ऊचिवान् तत्र सुयोधनेन । प्रवृद्धकोपस्फुरिताधरेण । असत्कृतः सत्स्पृहणीयशीलः । क्षत्ता सकर्णानुजसौबलेन ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: iti ūcivān tatra suyodhanena pravṛddhakopasphuritādhareṇa asatkṛtaḥ satspṛhaṇīyaśīlaḥ kṣattā sakarṇānujasaubalena

translation: (When) Vidura whose character was praiseworthy to the righteous, spoke thus in that assembly, he was insulted by Suyodhana whose lower lip was throbbing through great rage, and also by Karṇa, his younger brother (Duḥśāsana) and Śakuni.

devanagari: क एनमत्रोपजुहाव जिह्मं । दास्याः सुतं यद्‍बलिनैव पुष्टः । तस्मिन् प्रतीपः परकृत्य आस्ते । निर्वास्यतामाशु पुराच्छ्वसानः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: ka enamatropajuhāva jihmaṃ dāsyāḥ sutaṃ yad‍balinaiva puṣṭaḥ tasmin pratīpaḥ parakṛtya āste nirvāsyatāmāśu purācchvasānaḥ

translation: (Duryodhana said): “Who asked this crooked slave- born to be here? He is treacherous to his master with whose food he grew fat, and espouses the cause of the enemy. Let him be immediately driven out of the town alive[3] (confiscating his property.)”
purport:
Footnote [3]:
v.l. Śmaśānaḥ [Śmaśāna]—(i) inauspicious like a funeral place—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā
(ii) So evil that he will bring misfortune wherever he goes.

devanagari: स इत्थमत्युल्बणकर्णबाणैः । भ्रातुः पुरो मर्मसु ताडितोऽपि । स्वयं धनुर्द्वारि निधाय मायां । गतव्यथोऽयादुरु मानयानः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: sa itthamatyulbaṇakarṇabāṇaiḥ bhrātuḥ puro marmasu tāḍito svayaṃ dhanurdvāri nidhāya māyāṃ gatavyatho
translation: Though, in the very presence of his brother Dhṛta- rāṣṭra, he (Vidura) was cut to the quick by such sharp words which pierced the ears like arrows, he was unafflicted, as he well understood (and respected) the power of māyā (as the cause of this insult). Of his own accord, he left the assembly hall, leaving his bow at the door (indicating his neutrality in the ensuing struggle).

devanagari: स निर्गतः कौरवपुण्यलब्धो । गजाह्वयात् तीर्थपदः पदानि । अन्वाक्रमत्पुण्यचिकीर्षयोर्व्यां । स्वधिष्ठितो यानि सहस्रमूर्तिः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: sa nirgataḥ kauravapuṇyalabdho gajāhvayāt tīrthapadaḥ padāni anvākramatpuṇyacikīrṣayorvyāṃ svadhiṣṭhito yāni sahasramūrtiḥ
translation: He, whom Kauravas got by their good luck, departed from Hastināpura (along with good fortunes of the Kauravas). With a desire to accumulate merit, he visited, one after another, holy places sanctified by the sacred feet of Hari—holy places where God has manifested himself in thousand forms (like Śiva, Brahmā etc.).

devanagari: पुरेषु पुण्योपवनाद्रिकुञ्जे । ष्वपङ्कतोयेषु सरित्सरःसु । अनन्तलिङ्गैः समलङ्कृतेषु । चचार तीर्थायतनेष्वनन्यः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: pureṣu puṇyopavanādrikuñje ṣvapaṅkatoyeṣu saritsaraḥsu anantaliṅgaiḥ samalaṅkṛteṣu cacāra tīrthāyataneṣvananyaḥ

[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Unaccompanied by anyone, he travelled to towns, holy groves, mountains, bowers, rivers and lakes of translucent waters, and holy places and temples which were richly adorned with the symbols of the Infinite Lord.

devanagari: गां पर्यटन् मेध्यविविक्तवृत्तिः । सदाप्लुतोऽधः शयनोऽवधूतः । अलक्षितः स्वैरवधूतवेषो । व्रतानि चेरे हरितोषणानि ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: gāṃ paryaṭan medhyaviviktavṛttiḥ sadāpluto alakṣitaḥ svairavadhūtaveṣo vratāni cere haritoṣaṇāni

[imperfect active second plural], [imperfect middle third single]

translation: While wandering over the earth, he practised observances (vows) pleasing to Hari. He lived on pure consecrated food, performing ablutions in every sacred place, sleeping on the bare ground. Wearing bark garments, he was careless about his person, and became unrecognizable to his friends and relatives.

devanagari: इत्थं व्रजन् भारतमेव वर्षं । कालेन यावद्‍गतवान् प्रभासम् । तावच्छशास क्षितिमेक चक्रां । एकातपत्रामजितेन पार्थः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: itthaṃ vrajan bhāratameva varṣaṃ kālena yāvad‍gatavān prabhāsam tāvacchaśāsa kṣitimeka cakrāṃ ekātapatrāmajitena pārthaḥ

translation: In this way, while wandering in the Bhāratavarṣa, he went to Prabhāsa in due course. At that time, with the help of Kṛṣṇa, Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Pṛthā, was ruling over the whole earth which was (controlled) by the army of one king only (i.e. of Yudhiṣṭhira), and was under (the protection of) one royal umbrella.

devanagari: तत्राथ शुश्राव सुहृद्विनष्टिं । वनं यथा वेणुज वह्निसंश्रयम् । संस्पर्धया दग्धमथानुशोचन् । सरस्वतीं प्रत्यगियाय तूष्णीम् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: tatrātha śuśrāva suhṛdvinaṣṭiṃ vanaṃ yathā veṇuja vahnisaṃśrayam saṃspardhayā dagdhamathānuśocan sarasvatīṃ pratyagiyāya tūṣṇīm

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ śuc class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ śuc class 1 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Then, at that place, he heard (the report of) the annihilation of his friends (relatives) who were burnt down like a forest of dry bamboos consumed by conflagration. Then being deeply grieved, he returned to the Sarasvatī.

devanagari: तस्यां त्रितस्योशनसो मनोश्च । पृथोरथाग्नेरसितस्य वायोः । तीर्थं सुदासस्य गवां गुहस्य । यत् श्राद्धदेवस्य स आसिषेवे ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyāṃ tritasyośanaso manośca pṛthorathāgnerasitasya vāyoḥ tīrthaṃ sudāsasya gavāṃ guhasya yat śrāddhadevasya sa āsiṣeve

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [genitive single from √ ras class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ ras class 10 verb]
[genitive single from √ ras class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ ras class 10 verb]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: In the Sarasvatī, he resorted to places sacred to Trita, Uśanas, Manu, Pṛthu, Agni, Asita, Vāyu, Sudās, cows, Guha and Śrāddhadeva.

devanagari: अन्यानि चेह द्विजदेवदेवैः । कृतानि नानायतनानि विष्णोः । प्रत्यङ्ग मुख्याङ्कितमन्दिराणि । यद्दर्शनात् कृष्णमनुस्मरन्ति ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: anyāni ceha dvijadevadevaiḥ kṛtāni nānāyatanāni viṣṇoḥ pratyaṅga mukhyāṅkitamandirāṇi yaddarśanāt kṛṣṇamanusmaranti

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative plural from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ smṛ class 1 verb]

translation: In this region, he visited various temples of Viṣṇu constructed by sages and gods—temples which were especially distinguished by the most important of Viṣṇu’s weapons, viz., the disc Sudarśana (carved in the gold tops of the temples) the sight of which reminds people of Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: ततस्त्वतिव्रज्य सुराष्ट्रमृद्धं । सौवीरमत्स्यान् कुरुजाङ्गलांश्च । कालेन तावद्यमुनामुपेत्य । तत्रोद्धवं भागवतं ददर्श ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: tatastvativrajya surāṣṭramṛddhaṃ sauvīramatsyān kurujāṅgalāṃśca kālena tāvadyamunāmupetya tatroddhavaṃ bhāgavataṃ dadarśa

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Passing through the rich countries of the Surāṣṭras, the Sauvīras, the Matsyas, the Kurujāṅgalas, he reached the Yamunā where he met Uddhaya, the great devotee of the lord Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: स वासुदेवानुचरं प्रशान्तं । बृहस्पतेः प्राक्तनयं प्रतीतम् । आलिङ्ग्य गाढं प्रणयेन भद्रं । स्वानां अपृच्छद् भागवत्प्रजानाम् ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: sa vāsudevānucaraṃ praśāntaṃ bṛhaspateḥ prāktanayaṃ pratītam āliṅgya gāḍhaṃ praṇayena bhadraṃ svānāṃ apṛcchad bhāgavatprajānām
translation: With great affection, he closely embraced Uddhava that famous follower of Vāsudeva and a former pupil of Bṛhaspati (in politics), and who was perfectly serene in mind. He enquired of him about the welfare of his relatives who were under the protection of the Supreme Lord Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: कच्चित्पुराणौ पुरुषौ स्वनाभ्य । पाद्मानुवृत्त्येह किलावतीर्णौ । आसात उर्व्याः कुशलं विधाय । कृतक्षणौ कुशलं शूरगेहे ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: kaccitpurāṇau puruṣau svanābhya pādmānuvṛttyeha kilāvatīrṇau āsāta urvyāḥ kuśalaṃ vidhāya kṛtakṣaṇau kuśalaṃ śūragehe
translation: “Are the two ancient Puruṣas (Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma) well in the house of Śūrasena—the Puruṣas who incarnated on the earth due to the request of Brahmā born from the lotus in his (Viṣṇu’s) navel and who have enjoyed the joy of achieving the welfare of the world.

devanagari: कच्चित् कुरूणां परमः सुहृन्नो । भामः स आस्ते सुखमङ्ग शौरिः । यो वै स्वसॄणां पितृवद् ददाति । वरान् वदान्यो वरतर्पणेन ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: kaccit kurūṇāṃ paramaḥ suhṛnno bhāmaḥ sa āste sukhamaṅga śauriḥ yo vai svasṝṇāṃ pitṛvad dadāti varān vadānyo varatarpaṇena

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]
[nominative single from √ vṛ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ vṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ vṛ class 1 verb]

translation: Is the intimate friend of the Kurus, the respected Vasudeva, happy?—The liberal Vasudeva who like a father, gives sumptuous gifts to his sisters, in addition to the rich gifts given to the satisfaction of their husbands.

devanagari: कच्चिद् वरूथाधिपतिर्यदूनां । प्रद्युम्न आस्ते सुखमङ्ग वीरः । यं रुक्मिणी भगवतोऽभिलेभे । आराध्य विप्रान् स्मरमादिसर्गे ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: kaccid varūthādhipatiryadūnāṃ pradyumna āste sukhamaṅga vīraḥ yaṃ rukmiṇī bhagavato ārādhya viprān smaramādisarge

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Dear friend! Is Pradyumna, the brave commanderin-chief of the Yādava forces, hale and hearty? By propitiating Brāhmaṇas, Rukmiṇī, begot him (Pradyumna), the God of Love in his previous birth, as a son from Lord Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: कच्चित्सुखं सात्वतवृष्णिभोज । दाशार्हकाणामधिपः स आस्ते । यमभ्यषिञ्चत् शतपत्रनेत्रो । नृपासनाशां परिहृत्य दूरात् ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: kaccitsukhaṃ sātvatavṛṣṇibhoja dāśārhakāṇāmadhipaḥ sa āste yamabhyaṣiñcat śatapatranetro nṛpāsanāśāṃ parihṛtya dūrāt
translation: Is Ugrasena, the king of Sāttvatas, Vṛṣṇis, Bhojas and Dāśārhas happy?—Ugrasena whom the lotus-eyed ( Kṛṣṇa) installed as a king when he (through fear of his life) gave up the hopes about kingship.

devanagari: कच्चिद् हरेः सौम्य सुतः सदृक्ष । आस्तेऽग्रणी रथिनां साधु साम्बः । असूत यं जाम्बवती व्रताढ्या । देवं गुहं योऽम्बिकया धृतोऽग्रे ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: kaccid hareḥ saumya sutaḥ sadṛkṣa āste asūta yaṃ jāmbavatī vratāḍhyā devaṃ guhaṃ yo
translation: Oh gentle Uddhava! Is Sāmba, the son of Kṛṣṇa, and similar to him, the chief of warriors who ride chariots in battles, happy? Is the god Kārttikeya whom Pārvatī bore in the former birth and whom Jāmbavatī gave birth (as a son) after performing many severe observances, happy?

devanagari: क्षेमं स कच्चिद् युयुधान आस्ते । यः फाल्गुनात् लब्धधनूरहस्यः । लेभेऽञ्जसाधोक्षजसेवयैव । गतिं तदीयां यतिभिर्दुरापाम् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: kṣemaṃ sa kaccid yuyudhāna āste yaḥ phālgunāt labdhadhanūrahasyaḥ lebhe gatiṃ tadīyāṃ yatibhirdurāpām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ yudh class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ yudh class 4 verb]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[imperative active second single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: Is Sātyaki who learnt the secret technique of archery from Arjuna happy?—Sātyaki who by his intensely devoted service of Lord Kṛṣṇa attained the path leading to the Lord immediately,—a path which is difficult even for recluses to attain.

devanagari: कच्चिद् बुधः स्वस्त्यनमीव । आस्ते श्वफल्कपुत्रो भगवत्प्रपन्नः । यः कृष्णपादाङ्कितमार्गपांसु । ष्वचेष्टत प्रेमविभिन्नधैर्यः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: kaccid budhaḥ svastyanamīva āste śvaphalkaputro bhagavatprapannaḥ yaḥ kṛṣṇapādāṅkitamārgapāṃsu ṣvaceṣṭata premavibhinnadhairyaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second plural], [imperfect middle third single]

translation: Is the wise, pious (sinless) Akrūra (the son of Śva- phalka) always devoted to the glorious Lord, happy?—Akrūra, who lost himself due to his love (for God) and rolled himself in the dust on the road which was adorned with the foot-prints of Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: कच्चिच्छिवं देवकभोजपुत्र्या । विष्णुप्रजाया इव देवमातुः । या वै स्वगर्भेण दधार देवं । त्रयी यथा यज्ञवितानमर्थम् ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: kaccicchivaṃ devakabhojaputryā viṣṇuprajāyā iva devamātuḥ yā vai svagarbheṇa dadhāra devaṃ trayī yathā yajñavitānamartham

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Is Devakī, the princess of Devaka of Bhoja clan, happy?—Devakī, who like the mother of gods (Aditi) of whom Viṣṇu was born, verily bore the Lord in her womb like the Veda triad (Ṛg, Yajus and Sāma Vedas) which contains the meaning which expands into a sacrifice.

devanagari: अपिस्विदास्ते भगवान् सुखं वो । यः सात्वतां कामदुघोऽनिरुद्धः । यमामनन्ति स्म हि शब्दयोनिं । मनोमयं सत्त्वतुरीयतत्त्वम् ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: apisvidāste bhagavān sukhaṃ vo yaḥ sātvatāṃ kāmadugho yamāmananti sma hi śabdayoniṃ manomayaṃ sattvaturīyatattvam
translation: Is the revered Aniruddha happy?—(Aniruddha)who fulfills the desires of the sāttvatas and who is regarded as the source of Śāstras by the Vedas, as the director of the mind, for he is the fourth principle presiding over sattva or antaḥ-karaṇa (the inner organ)[4].
purport:
Footnote [4]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā explains: citta (reason), ahaṃkāra (self-sense or ego), buddhi (intelligence) and manas (mind) are four aspects of antaḥ-karaṇa (the inner organ) and Vāsudeva, Saṅkarṣaṇa, Pradyumna and Aniruddha are their respective presiding deities. Hence Aniruddha is called here ‘director of the mind’ and the fourth principle (deity) governing antaḥkaraṇa (the internal organ).

devanagari: अपिस्विदन्ये च निजात्मदैवं । अनन्यवृत्त्या समनुव्रता ये । हृदीकसत्यात्मज चारुदेष्ण । गदादयः स्वस्ति चरन्ति सौम्य ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: apisvidanye ca nijātmadaivaṃ ananyavṛttyā samanuvratā ye hṛdīkasatyātmaja cārudeṣṇa gadādayaḥ svasti caranti saumya
translation: Oh gentle Uddhava! Are also Hṛdika, Sātyaki, Cārudeṣṇa, Gada and others, well?—those who in their absolute devotion to Kṛṣṇa worship him as the Lord of their Soul which is different from their bodies (etc.)

devanagari: अपि स्वदोर्भ्यां विजयाच्युताभ्यां । धर्मेण धर्मः परिपाति सेतुम् । दुर्योधनोऽतप्यत यत्सभायां । साम्राज्यलक्ष्म्या विजयानुवृत्त्या ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: api svadorbhyāṃ vijayācyutābhyāṃ dharmeṇa dharmaḥ paripāti setum duryodhano sāmrājyalakṣmyā vijayānuvṛttyā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect middle third single], [imperfect passive third single]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Is Dharma (Yudhiṣṭhira), with two arms, viz. Arjuna and Kṛṣṇa, protecting according to the duties of kings the established (socio-religious) institutions?—(Yudhiṣṭhira) in whose assembly, Duryodhana boiled (with rage and jealousy) at the sight of Arjuna’s services and the imperial fortune gained (by him) through victories.

devanagari: किं वा कृताघेष्वघमत्यमर्षी । भीमोऽहिवद्दीर्घतमं व्यमुञ्चत् । यस्याङ्घ्रिपातं रणभूर्न सेहे । मार्गं गदायाश्चरतो विचित्रम् ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: kiṃ vā kṛtāgheṣvaghamatyamarṣī bhīmo yasyāṅghripātaṃ raṇabhūrna sehe mārgaṃ gadāyāścarato vicitram

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]
[vocative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

[imperative active second plural], [injunctive middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Or has Bhīma who is highly intolerant with the wrongdoers and nurtured rage (revenge) like a serpent, given it up (or visited it on the offenders—Kauravas)?—(Bhīma) whose (heavy) steps the earth could not bear, while he moved about in various ways, brandishing his mace (gadā).

devanagari: कच्चिद् यशोधा रथयूथपानां । गाण्डीव धन्वोपरतारिरास्ते । अलक्षितो यच्छरकूटगूढो । मायाकिरातो गिरिशस्तुतोष ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: kaccid yaśodhā rathayūthapānāṃ gāṇḍīva dhanvoparatārirāste alakṣito yaccharakūṭagūḍho māyākirāto giriśastutoṣa

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Is Arjuna, the celebrated warrior among the leaders of armies of chariots, the wielder of the Gāṇḍīva bow, enemyless (after destroying them all)?—(Arjuna) with whom was pleased god Śiva who assumed the form of a Kirāta, at his (Arjuna) covering him thickly with volleys of arrows.[5]
purport:
Footnote [5]:
This refers to the test of Arjuna’s heroism taken by god Śiva before granting him the Pāśupata missile (Vide Mahābhārata, II.39.32-64.)

devanagari: यमावुतस्वित्तनयौ पृथायाः । पार्थैर्वृतौ पक्ष्मभिरक्षिणीव । रेमात उद्दाय मृधे स्वरिक्थं । परात्सुपर्णाविव वज्रिवक्त्रात् ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: yamāvutasvittanayau pṛthāyāḥ pārthairvṛtau pakṣmabhirakṣiṇīva remāta uddāya mṛdhe svarikthaṃ parātsuparṇāviva vajrivaktrāt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ vṛ class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ vṛ class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ vṛ class 1 verb], [nominative dual from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [vocative dual from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [accusative dual from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [nominative dual from √ vṛ class 9 verb], [vocative dual from √ vṛ class 9 verb], [accusative dual from √ vṛ class 9 verb]

[nominative dual from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [vocative dual from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [accusative dual from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [nominative dual from √ vṛ class 9 verb], [vocative dual from √ vṛ class 9 verb], [accusative dual from √ vṛ class 9 verb]

translation: Are the twins (who were brought up as) sons of Pṛthā (Kuntī), who are protected (lit. surrounded) by Kuntī’s sons like eyes by the eyelids, enjoying themselves after wresting their share of ancestral kingdom from the enemies in the war, like Garuḍa taking (the share of) nectar from the army of gods?[6]
purport:
Footnote [6]:
vajri-vaktra—From the moutḥ of Indra (Bhāvāratha Dīpikā, Bhāgavata Candrikā, Padaratnāvalī) But Bhāvārtha-dīpikā-prakāśa objects that a thing taken from the mouth of another is unacceptable. Hence he dissolves the compound: vajrī vaktram (pradhānaṃ) yasya / ‘That of which Indra is the leader!’

devanagari: अहो पृथापि ध्रियतेऽर्भकार्थे । राजर्षिवर्येण विनापि तेन । यस्त्वेकवीरोऽधिरथो विजिग्ये । धनुर्द्वितीयः ककुभश्चतस्रः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: aho pṛthāpi dhriyate rājarṣivaryeṇa vināpi tena yastvekavīro dhanurdvitīyaḥ kakubhaścatasraḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: How wonderful, that though bereaved of a great royal sage (like Pāṇḍu), a matchless warrior, who seated in his chariot with only his bow as a helpmate, conquered all the four cardinal points, Kuntī stayed alive for the sake of children!

devanagari: सौम्यानुशोचे तमधःपतन्तं । भ्रात्रे परेताय विदुद्रुहे यः । निर्यापितो येन सुहृत्स्वपुर्या । अहं स्वपुत्रान् समनुव्रतेन ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: saumyānuśoce tamadhaḥpatantaṃ bhrātre paretāya vidudruhe yaḥ niryāpito yena suhṛtsvapuryā ahaṃ svaputrān samanuvratena

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active third plural], [aorist active third plural]

translation: Oh milḍ-natured Uddhava! I feel sorry for the downfalling Dhṛtarāṣṭra who showed enmity to his departed brother (Pāṇḍu). I (his living brother and), a friend and well- wisher was expelled from his capital by him who followed his sons.

devanagari: सोऽहं हरेर्मर्त्यविडम्बनेन । दृशो नृणां चालयतो विधातुः । नान्योपलक्ष्यः पदवीं प्रसादात् । चरामि पश्यन् गतविस्मयोऽत्र ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: so dṛśo nṛṇāṃ cālayato vidhātuḥ nānyopalakṣyaḥ padavīṃ prasādāt carāmi paśyan gatavismayo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ cal ], [ablative single from √ cal ], [genitive single from √ cal ]
[ablative single from √ cal ], [genitive single from √ cal ]
[nominative single from √ lakṣ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ lakṣ class 10 verb]

translation: (Though thus maltreated), I felt no wonder as I Could see the greatness of Hari who confuses the working of the human mind by his assumption of human form; and, being in disguise, I could leisurely perform pilgrimage on the earth.

devanagari: नूनं नृपाणां त्रिमदोत्पथानां । महीं मुहुश्चालयतां चमूभिः । वधात्प्रपन्नार्तिजिहीर्षयेशोऽपि । उपैक्षताघं भगवान् कुरूणाम् ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: nūnaṃ nṛpāṇāṃ trimadotpathānāṃ mahīṃ muhuścālayatāṃ camūbhiḥ vadhātprapannārtijihīrṣayeśo upaikṣatāghaṃ bhagavān kurūṇām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect middle third single], [aorist middle third plural]

translation: Though the venerable Lord is powerful enough to remove the miseries of his devotees, by destroying the kings who were proud of their triple superiority (viz. in learning, riches and heredity), and who constantly made the earth tremble by their armies, he was indifferent to the sinful conduct of the Kauravas.

devanagari: अजस्य जन्मोत्पथनाशनाय । कर्माण्यकर्तुर्ग्रहणाय पुंसाम् । नन्वन्यथा कोऽर्हति देहयोगं । परो गुणानामुत कर्मतंत्रम् ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: ajasya janmotpathanāśanāya karmāṇyakarturgrahaṇāya puṃsām nanvanyathā ko paro guṇānāmuta karmataṃtram
translation: The incarnation of the unborn god is for the destruction of those who go astray. Though unconcerned with actions, his deeds are reclaiming the erranṭs to the proper path. If (that be) not (the case) who, (being) above guṇas, would verily like the assumption of the body and the course of activities.

devanagari: तस्य प्रपन्नाखिललोकपानां । अवस्थितानां अनुशासने स्वे । अर्थाय जातस्य यदुष्वजस्य । वार्तां सखे कीर्तय तीर्थकीर्तेः ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: tasya prapannākhilalokapānāṃ avasthitānāṃ anuśāsane sve arthāya jātasya yaduṣvajasya vārtāṃ sakhe kīrtaya tīrthakīrteḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [genitive single from √ jan class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ jan class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ jan class 3 verb], [genitive single from √ jan class 4 verb]
[genitive single from √ jan class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ jan class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ jan class 3 verb], [genitive single from √ jan class 4 verb]

translation:
Oh friend! Extol to me the news (episodes) of the unborn Lord of Holy Fame, who was born in the Yadu race for the good of the kings of the world who have submitted themselves to him (his protection) and for the welfare of those who abide by his orders.
